title: Loxanthes humilis
intro: Loxanthes humilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae, commonly found in the Andes mountains. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Loxanthes humilis include iridoid glycosides, such as gentiopicroside and swertiamarin, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Loxanthes humilis is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments.
taxonomy: Loxanthes humilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It belongs to the genus Loxanthes, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The plant is native to the high-altitude grasslands and rocky outcrops of the Andes mountain range. The classification of L. humilis is that of a perennial herb, with a compact growth habit and small, delicate flowers. As a member of the Gentianaceae family, L. humilis is closely related to other plants in the same family, such as gentians and swertia.
